content.method: Cohort-projection modeling using existing birth-rate and enrollment administrative data, combined with capacity-utilization analysis of dormitory (kollégium) infrastructure across regions, disaggregated by settlement type.
content.question: What is the current and projected enrollment trajectory for 6/8-year gimnazium places relative to a declining student cohort over the next 5-10 years, and how would this affect selectivity ratios, dormitory utilization, and the marginal cost/benefit of structural reform versus natural attrition?
content.why_it_matters: Demographic contraction may force de facto network changes independent of policy choice; understanding this trajectory clarifies whether T1 (status quo/monitoring) is a passive-but-consequential choice, and whether reform costs (school closures, teacher redeployment) invoked against T2/T6 are overstated given already-anticipated capacity shifts.
